description: A question uses 'kua' for its missing place or location. Physical and figurative locations are both possible when the rest of the clause supports them. A source or destination keeps its ordinary preposition, so 'kua' alone does not also mean from where or to where.
articulatory_notes: Make an unvoiced closure by pressing the tongue's rear against the soft palate, then release directly into high-back rounded 'u'. After that first syllable, drop the jaw for a separate 'a' in the open-central position. Emphasis rests on 'ku'.
sound_symbolism: Releasing 'k' sends the mouth from the compact shape of rounded 'u' to the broad opening of separate 'a'. That widening can feel like a small search across space.
usage_notes: 'kua' occupies the place phrase's position. There is no accompanying 'wa' (Q). The preposition remains before the gap: 'lue' (ABL) gives 'lue kua', from where, while 'kau' (ALL) gives 'kau kua', to what destination.
